claimText: Off-budget borrowing refers to government borrowing that is not explicitly included in the official budget and is not approved by the legislature.
evidenceExcerpt: Off-budget borrowing is not explicitly included in the official budget or approved by the legislature.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and legislative oversight of off-budget borrowings.
distractorHooks: Requires direct legislative approval prior to borrowing

claimText: Off-budget borrowings are typically raised through government-controlled Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) or entities dependent on budgetary support.
evidenceExcerpt: Raised through government-controlled PSUs or entities dependent on budgetary support.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the vehicles used for off-budget borrowing.
distractorHooks: Raised directly by the Ministry of Finance
Raised exclusively through foreign sovereign bonds

claimText: The purpose of off-budget borrowing is to keep borrowings off the fiscal deficit books, which artificially creates an impression of fiscal prudence.
evidenceExcerpt: Keeps borrowings off the fiscal deficit books to create an impression of fiscal prudence.
examUse: Conceptual question on how off-budget borrowing affects reported fiscal deficit figures.
distractorHooks: Is fully accounted for in the FRBM targets

claimText: Off-budget borrowing reduces fiscal transparency, lacks accountability, and conceals the true debt burden of the government.
evidenceExcerpt: Concerns include reduced transparency, lack of accountability, and concealment of the true debt burden.
examUse: Statement-based question on the macroeconomic implications and risks of off-budget borrowing.
distractorHooks: Reduces the actual debt burden of the government
